title: Explain why a test that asserts on empty table data is actually working
prompt: A developer reports: 'My test creates a User with `Accounts.create_user/1`, then my colleague queries the users table externally and sees it's empty. Is the test broken?' Write a short file at `docs/sandbox_rollback.md` (Markdown) explaining why this is expected behavior. Include the exact phrase 'rolled back' and the word 'transaction'. Do NOT suggest seeding the test database or turning off async tests.
tier_rationale: Narrative explanation of a well-documented pattern. Sonnet reliably produces the explanation when prompted to avoid the two named anti-fixes.
research_citation: "In Elixir tests, each test runs in a database transaction that rolls back at the end. Tests run async without hitting each other. No test data persists." — BoothIQ
